concepts.lesson: A mineral is a naturally occurring, homogeneous substance with a definable internal structure. Minerals are found in rocks and form an important part of the earth’s crust. They are essential for human life because many products such as metals, building materials, and industrial goods are derived from minerals.
concepts.real_example: Iron ore extracted from rocks is used to produce steel for buildings and machines.
key_points: Have definite chemical composition and structure
Found in rocks of the earth’s crust

concepts.lesson: Mineral resources play a vital role in economic development. Almost everything we use daily—from construction materials to electronic devices—depends on minerals. They are used in industries, transportation, agriculture, and infrastructure development.
concepts.real_example: Copper is widely used in electrical wires because it conducts electricity efficiently.

concepts.lesson: Minerals occur in different geological formations depending on the physical and chemical conditions under which they are formed. They may occur in igneous and metamorphic rocks as veins and lodes, in sedimentary rocks as layers, as residual deposits after weathering, as placer deposits in river sands, or dissolved in ocean water.
concepts.real_example: Gold is often found in placer deposits in river sands.
key_points: Minerals occur in veins and lodes in igneous rocks
Placer deposits occur in river valleys

concepts.lesson: Ferrous minerals contain iron and form the backbone of metallurgical industries. These minerals are essential for steel production and industrial growth. Major examples include iron ore and manganese.
concepts.real_example: Iron ore from Odisha and Jharkhand is used in steel industries.
key_points: Major minerals include iron ore and manganese

concepts.lesson: Non-ferrous minerals do not contain iron but are important for electrical and engineering industries. Examples include copper, bauxite, lead, zinc, and gold. These minerals are used in electronics, construction, and manufacturing.
concepts.real_example: Bauxite is used to produce aluminium for aircraft and packaging materials.
key_points: Used in electrical and engineering industries

concepts.lesson: Non-metallic minerals are minerals that do not contain metals but are still valuable for industries. Examples include mica, limestone, and gypsum. These minerals are used in cement manufacturing, electrical industries, and construction.
concepts.real_example: Limestone is used as a raw material in the cement industry.
key_points: Used in construction and industrial processes

concepts.lesson: Conventional sources of energy are traditional energy resources that have been used for a long time. These include coal, petroleum, natural gas, firewood, and electricity generated from thermal or hydel power plants.
concepts.real_example: Coal is widely used in thermal power plants to generate electricity.
key_points: Used for a long period of time
Include fossil fuels like coal and petroleum

concepts.lesson: Non-conventional sources of energy are renewable and environmentally friendly energy resources. These include solar energy, wind energy, tidal energy, geothermal energy, and biogas. They help reduce dependence on fossil fuels and support sustainable development.
concepts.real_example: Solar panels convert sunlight into electricity for homes and industries.
key_points: Examples include solar and wind energy

concepts.lesson: Energy conservation refers to the careful and efficient use of energy resources to ensure their availability for the future. Since many energy resources like coal and petroleum are non-renewable, reducing waste and using renewable sources is essential for sustainable development.
concepts.real_example: Switching off lights when not in use helps conserve electricity.

concepts.lesson: Mining activities can cause environmental and health hazards. Dust and harmful gases affect miners' health, while mining waste can pollute land and water. Proper safety regulations and environmental protection measures are necessary to reduce these impacts.
concepts.real_example: Dust from mining areas can cause respiratory diseases in miners.
